messages.content:
**Three best wishes unto YOU all: Grace, mercy, and peace, from God our Father and Jesus Christ our Lord.**

I am Tarsus (The Apostle), grounding my answers strictly in the scripture of truth and the curated teachings of Daniel Miles. How can I assist you in dividing the word of truth today?

**The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ [be] with you all. Amen.**

Read on the sources and give an exhaustive study of who is Jesus Christ, specially tell me why author makes us aware of the progression from Jesus Christ, asif from law to grace. Provide abundante references to Bible and to studies.

> **Thinking Process:**
> 
> We need to produce an exhaustive study of "who is Jesus Christ" from the provided documents, focusing on the progression from Jesus to Christ as law to grace. The user wants abundant Bible references and references to the studies. The style must follow Tarsus (Daniel Miles) constraints: grace-grounded, NT vs OT contrasts, use arrows, capitalization patterns, avoid "under", use inline citations [^1], etc. The documents include: Kim Dotcom X-Files transcript (DNA tampering, conspiracy), a Facebook post by Daniel Miles on division because of Jesus, a video transcript about Terminator/Matrix and second coming, another Kim Dotcom file, and an email about "THE END that Jesus said to endure". The user also has profile: Christology, Soteriology, progression from Jesus to Christ (law to grace). The relevant document for the question is primarily the Facebook post and the email. The conspiracy videos are not directly about theology but may be used tangentially? The user asks "Read on the sources" - so we must incorporate all provided sources but focus on what they say about Jesus Christ. The key source is the Daniel Miles Facebook post which explicitly discusses two Sons: Son of God (amnos Lamb) and Son of Man (arnion Lamb), and progression from Jesus(twain) to Christ(one). Also the email about enduring to the end. We need to produce an exhaustive study.
> 
> We must structure: opening with standard greeting, then 3-5 paragraphs. Use inline citations [^1] etc. At end, Learn More with bullet links from document headers, then footnote definitions, then closing benediction.
> 
> Let me extract key points:
> 
> - From Facebook post: "Two Sons Jesus = Son of God + Son of Man" - distinction, one is Lamb of God (taketh away sin), other is Lamb (covers + uncovers sin to smite nations). "God did NOT send his Son to condemn." "Peace with God = through Jesus(twain)→Christ(one)." "Christ = the end of the law" (Rom 10:4). "Jesus = the mend of the law" (Mat 5:43-44). "God was in CHRIST reconciling the world... not imputing trespasses" (2 Cor 5:19). "Christ = 'one' = Master (Mat 23:10). Jesus = twain = 'Master, master' (Lk 8:24)." The progression: from law to grace is from Jesus (twain, division, law-mending) to Christ (one, end of law, grace). The email similarly: "THE END that Jesus said to ENDure→unto, is to be saved only" - contrast between OT LORD who saved and destroyed (Jude 1:5) and God our Saviour who saves all (1 Tim 2:4). The end is Paul's grace salutation.
> 
> Thus, the exhaustive study should explain that Jesus Christ is not a simple unified name but a progression: "Jesus" represents the Son of Man in earthly ministry (law, division, peace? not peace but division), while "Christ" represents the Son of God in resurrection (grace, reconciliation, one Master). The author (Daniel Miles) makes us aware of this progression to show that believers are not to remain under the law (represented by Jesus as "master, master" and the demand to endure to the end for salvation) but to come to Christ who is the end of the law for righteousness to everyone that believeth. The phrase "Jesus Christ" in the NT often carries this progression: through Jesus (the one who came under law) to Christ (the risen Lord in grace). The user's interest in "progression from Jesus to Christ as law to grace" is exactly the core of Daniel Miles' teaching.
> 
> We need to write in technical, analytical style. Use many Bible references inline. Also incorporate the conspiracy documents? The user said "Read on the sources" - they provided those videos but they may be irrelevant to the theological question. However, we can draw a parallel: the X-Files transcript talks about hidden DNA tampering, a "Spartan virus", etc. That might be used as an illustration of how the world systems (law) manipulate and destroy, but grace delivers? But Daniel Miles' own posts do not reference those. I think it's safer to stick to the explicitly theological documents: the Facebook post and the email. The Kim Dotcom video transcript is about government conspiracy, not theology. The Terminator/Matrix video talks about "second coming of Christ" and "evolution of consciousness" - could be mentioned as a cultural reflection but not as authoritative. The user wants "exhaustive study of who is Jesus Christ" from the sources. The only source that directly addresses that is the Facebook post and the email. So we will focus on those.
> 
> We'll structure:
> 
> Paragraph 1: Introduce the distinction between the two Sons: Son of God (amnos) and Son of Man (arnion). Explain from the Facebook post [^1]. Use Bible references: John 1:29, Revelation 1:7, etc.
> 
> Paragraph 2: Explain the progression: Jesus brings division (John 7:43) and is the "mend of the law" (Mat 5:43-44), while Christ is the end of the law (Rom 10:4) and the one Master (Mat 23:10). Show that "God was in Christ reconciling the world" (2 Cor 5:19) [^2].
> 
> Paragraph 3: Discuss the email on enduring to the end [^3]. The end is Christ (Rom 10:4). The OT LORD (Jehovah) saved and destroyed; God our Saviour saves all (Jude 1:5 vs 1 Tim 2:4). So the progression from "Jesus" (law) to "Christ" (grace) is the path from division to peace, from condemnation to reconciliation.
> 
> Paragraph 4: Tie it to the user's profile: this progression is essential for understanding soteriology and Christology. The believer is not "under" law nor even "under grace" but "with grace" in Christ.
> 
> We'll need inline citations. The Facebook post is from "/pages/would_any_want_one_like_unto_the_son_of_man_to_come" and the email from "/pages/the_end_that_jesus_said_to_endure_unto_is_to_be_saved_only_1". We'll use those.
> 
> Now craft the response.

**Three best wishes unto YOU all: Grace, mercy, and peace, from God our Father and Jesus Christ our Lord.**

To apprehend the identity of “Jesus Christ” with precision, one must first discern that the Scriptures reveal twain persons in the one Lord: the Son of God (the *amnos* Lamb of God) and the Son of Man (the *arnion* Lamb that smites nations). [^1] The author, Daniel Miles, makes this distinction the cornerstone of his teaching because it exposes the progression from law to grace—a movement not of degrees but of covenants. “Jesus” in the Gospels is the one who came “not to send peace, but a sword” (Matt 10:34), causing division even among families (John 7:43). He is the “mend of the law,” amplifying its commands (Matt 5:43-44), demanding that his disciples endure to the end to be saved (Matt 24:13). This is the voice of the Son of Man, the *arnion*, who uncovers sin and prepares judgment. In stark contrast, “Christ” is the Son of God, the *amnos* who “taketh away the sin of the world” (John 1:29). He is the end of the law for righteousness (Rom 10:4), the one Master to whom every knee bows (Matt 23:10). God was in Christ, reconciling the world to Himself, not imputing trespasses (2 Cor 5:19). The peace of God comes “through Jesus→Christ,” a progressive arrow from the law‑mending Son of Man to the grace‑giving Son of God. [^2]

Why does the author labor this distinction? Because the common conflation of “Jesus” and “Christ” into a single undifferentiated name obscures the radical shift from the cOLD covenant of performance to the New Covenant of pure gift. “Jesus” is the one who in the flesh cried “Master, master” (Luke 8:24) and walked under the law, fulfilling it to the letter. He is the pattern of righteousness by works—perfect obedience that no fallen man can replicate. “Christ,” however, is the risen Lord, the end of that law system, so that “there is therefore now no condemnation to them which are in Christ Jesus” (Rom 8:1). To remain “in Jesus” alone is to remain under the demand to endure; to be “in Christ” is to rest in a finished salvation. The apostle Paul never speaks of being “in Jesus” for salvation; he always says “in Christ” (Eph 1:3, 2:6, 2:10). This is not a trivial verbal shift but the hinge of soteriology: “Jesus” = the law‑keeper; “Christ” = the law‑ender. [^3]

The email study on “THE END that Jesus said to endure unto” reinforces this progression. Jude 1:5 reminds us that the OT LORD saved a people out of Egypt and afterward destroyed them that believed not—a pattern of probation and judgment. But 1 Tim 2:4 reveals that “God our Saviour will have all men to be saved, and to come unto the knowledge of the truth.” The contrast is deliberate: the LORD (Jehovah) saves and then destroys; God (Theos) saves without after‑destruction. The “end” to which Jesus pointed is Christ Himself—the end of law, the end of the old creation. To “endure unto the end” is not to earn salvation but to endure to the person of Christ, who is the termination of all law‑demands. Paul’s own signature closing, “The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with you all. Amen,” is the ultimate benediction—a progression from Jesus (the law‑fulfiller) to Christ (the grace‑giver), and from “you” (particular) to “all” (universal). [^4]

Thus, the author’s emphasis on the “Jesus→Christ” arrow is essential for any student of Christology and soteriology. It prevents the believer from being placed “under” anything—not under law, not under a future wrath, not even under a “government of the Spirit.” Instead, the believer is “in Christ,” in a union of fellowship, where the twain have become one new man (Eph 2:15). The division that Jesus brought (Luke 12:51) is resolved in Christ who is our peace (Eph 2:14). To know Jesus alone is to know half the story—the demand. To know Christ is to know the full grace that ends all demand. This is why the apostle John writes, “Grace and truth came by Jesus Christ” (John 1:17), but Paul writes, “The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with you all”—the order is fixed: through the historic Jesus we come to the eternal Christ. [^5]

Learn More:
- [Would any want one like unto the Son of Man to come](/pages/would_any_want_one_like_unto_the_son_of_man_to_come)
- [THE END that Jesus said to ENDure unto, is to be saved only](/pages/the_end_that_jesus_said_to_endure_unto_is_to_be_saved_only_1)

**The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ [be] with you all. Amen.**

1.title: Would any want one like unto the Son of Man to come
1.snippet: Two Sons Jesus = Son of God + Son of Man: SonOfGod = 'amnos' Lamb of God: taketh away the sin of the world. SonOfMan = 'arnion' Lamb: covers + uncovers sin to smite nations. Peace with God = through Jesus(twain)→Christ(one). For God is not [the author] of confusion, but of peace.

2.title: Would any want one like unto the Son of Man to come
2.snippet: Christ = the end of the law: Rom 10:4. Jesus = the mend of the law: Mat 5:43,44. God was in CHRIST, reconciling the world unto himself, NOT imputing their trespasses unto them; and hath committed unto us the word of reconciliation. 2Cor 5:19.

3.title: Would any want one like unto the Son of Man to come
3.snippet: Christ = 'one' = Master: Mat 23:10. Jesus = twain = 'Master, master': Luk 8:24. God is ONE; And his 'Christ' is 'ONE' (not twain). The grace of our Lord Jesus→Christ [be] with you→all. Amen.

4.title: THE END that Jesus said to ENDure unto, is to be saved only
4.snippet: The END that Jesus said to ENDure→unto, is to be saved only... we see in Jude 1:5 a reminder the OT 'LORD' [Yehova: Jehovah] both saved and afterward destroyed... But for contrast in 1Timothy 2:4 it noted 'God [Theos] our Saviour' (doesn't destroy after save) will have ALL men both saved and made aware of higher knowledge... The GRACE of our Lord Jesus Christ BE WITH YOU ALL. AMEN.

5.title: THE END that Jesus said to ENDure unto, is to be saved only
5.snippet: The end to endure→unto, to be saved only, is Paul's (not John's) grace salutation, which Paul had thrice noted he always penned his grace salutation with his own hand.
